index.html.title: IKE Workspace Extension
index.html.body:
IKE Workspace Extension [1] network.ike.tooling:ike-workspace-extension is a Maven 4 build extension that prunes non-existent <subprojects> entries from IKE workspace POMs before model validation. Lets a fresh clone of any IKE workspace bootstrap with mvn ws:scaffold-init even when no subproject directory is on disk yet. What it does When Maven 4 reads a workspace POM declaring several subprojects but the working tree only has the workspace root (because the operator just ran git clone and has not yet populated subprojects), Maven’s model validator fatally aborts the build with "Child subproject X does not exist" — before any goal can run. That makes the documented bootstrap (git clone && cd && mvn ws:scaffold-init) impossible. See IKE-Network/ike-issues#459[2]. This extension implements org.apache.maven.api.spi.ModelTransformer.transformFileModel, which fires before profile activation and subproject existence validation. For any POM whose directory has a sibling workspace.yaml (the "this is a workspace POM" marker), the transformer: Removes <subprojects> entries whose <sub>/pom.xml does not exist on disk. Removes file-activated profiles whose <file><exists> target is missing — so any -P?with-X flag on the command line silently no-ops on the missing profile name. Every other POM passes through untouched. Stability commitment This artifact does exactly one thing and never grows. The dependency surface stays at the Maven 4 API jars (compile scope, loaded into the extension classloader). No ike-* runtime dependencies, no business logic, no knowledge of IKE conventions beyond "sibling workspace.yaml marks a workspace POM." Versioned independently of the ike-platform[3] release cadence. If we ever need different behavior, we add a separate extension rather than evolving this one. Wiring Declared in a workspace’s .mvn/extensions.xml: <extensions>
    <extension>
        <groupId>network.ike.tooling</groupId>
        <artifactId>ike-workspace-extension</artifactId>
        <version>1</version>
    </extension>
</extensions> The ike-platform workspace plugin’s ws:scaffold-init writes this entry into new workspaces automatically; ws:scaffold-publish keeps it in sync on existing workspaces. The version is sourced from a property declared in ike-parent — operators do not maintain it by hand. Coordinates <dependency>
    <groupId>network.ike.tooling</groupId>
    <artifactId>ike-workspace-extension</artifactId>
    <version>1</version>
</dependency> Available from Maven Central. Tracking IKE-Network/ike-issues#460[4] — productionization plan IKE-Network/ike-issues#459[2] — underlying defect this extension fixes

release-notes.html.body: Release Notes ike-tooling v209 Internal Unify visual theme across Maven site, JaCoCo, and Javadoc (currently three different themes per project) (#518[1]) ike-tooling v208 Internal Configure maven-javadoc-plugin <links> for cross-module references across foundation apidocs (#517[2]) ike-base-parent v10 Internal Release ike-base-parent v10 to propagate ike-java-support v1→v2 canonical pin (#519[3]) ike-tooling v207 Internal Clean up stale release-cascade.yaml content (drop unread version-property data; update X.version comments) (#516[4]) Publish Javadoc on ike-tooling and ike-java-support Maven sites (#513[5]) ike-java-support v2 Internal ike-java-support is missing src/main/cascade/release-cascade.yaml (#515[6]) ike-tooling v206 Internal Landing page polish: Kroki dependency diagram + complete site/README for new foundation members (#511[7]) ike-tooling v198 Internal Async Maven Central deploy with sentinel-file status tracking (#484[8]) ike-tooling v196 Internal Nexus-first two-phase deploy with retries in ike:release-publish (#482[9]) ike-tooling v185 Internal Consolidate the AsciiDoc doc-rendering pipeline into ike-doc-maven-plugin (#437[10]) Add Central-required POM metadata (developers, scm); fix stale reactor comment (#434[11]) Re-pin koncept-asciidoc-extension to network.ike.docs groupId (#432[12]) ike-platform v68 Internal ws:scaffold-publish report: show parent-cascade from→to and post-run uncommitted state (#431[13]) ike-tooling v183 Internal URL-mode cascade resolver — assemble the release cascade without local sibling checkouts (#429[14]) Fail ike:release-publish on preflight warnings by default; add ike.release.ignoreWarnings (#428[15]) ike-tooling v182 Internal Decentralize the release cascade: per-project manifests, loosely coupled (#420[16]) Complete the ike:-tier release-cascade capability (executor, alignment, terminal marker, POM wiring) (#419[17]) ike-tooling v180 Fixes Foundation-drift report mislabels 'ahead' projects as 'behind'; no direction, no explanation (#412[18]) Internal Developer environment setup guide in ike-build-standards + scaffold-enforced README link (#410[19]) ike-platform v58 Fixes workspace.yaml: ws:checkpoint emits duplicate sha keys instead of replacing (#387[20]) Enhancements Document checkpoint vs release issue handling: checkpoints report, releases close (#394[21]) Collapse 15 workspace goals into 3 scaffold-* goals via convergence pattern (#393[22]) ike-tooling v175 Fixes IKE-WORKSPACE.md references archived network.ike.pipeline pluginGroup (#389[23]) Enhancements ike:* site lifecycle convergence: 7 goals → 2 (site-draft / site-publish) (#398[24]) Standards: every standards-change issue must include a Documentation Impact section (#396[25]) Release preflight: verify gh permissions and pending-release label setup for issue-management workflow (#392[26]) Release process should remove pending-release label from resolved issues (#390[27]) Build standards: add commit-message issue-association standard (#388[28]) ike-pipeline 111 Internal ike-pipeline: port to ike-tooling 127 — SubprojectType removal (#228[29])

dependency-info.html.title: Maven Coordinates
dependency-info.html.body:
Maven Coordinates Apache Maven <dependency>
  <groupId>network.ike.tooling</groupId>
  <artifactId>ike-workspace-extension</artifactId>
  <version>6</version>
</dependency> Apache Ivy <dependency org="network.ike.tooling" name="ike-workspace-extension" rev="6">
  <artifact name="ike-workspace-extension" type="jar" />
</dependency> Groovy Grape @Grapes(
@Grab(group='network.ike.tooling', module='ike-workspace-extension', version='6')
) Gradle/Grails implementation 'network.ike.tooling:ike-workspace-extension:6' Scala SBT libraryDependencies += "network.ike.tooling" % "ike-workspace-extension" % "6" Leiningen [network.ike.tooling/ike-workspace-extension "6"]

scm.html.title: Source Code Management
scm.html.body: Overview This project uses Git[1] to manage its source code. Instructions on Git use can be found at https://git-scm.com/doc[2]. Web Browser Access The following is a link to a browsable version of the source repository: https://github.com/IKE-Network/ike-workspace-extension[3] Anonymous Access The source can be checked out anonymously from Git with this command (See https://git-scm.com/docs/git-clone[4]): $ git clone https://github.com/IKE-Network/ike-workspace-extension.git Developer Access Only project developers can access the Git tree via this method (See https://git-scm.com/docs/git-clone[4]). $ git clone git@github.com:IKE-Network/ike-workspace-extension.git Access from Behind a Firewall Refer to the documentation of the SCM used for more information about access behind a firewall.

team.html.title: Project Team
team.html.body: Project Team A successful project requires many people to play many roles. Some members write code or documentation, while others are valuable as testers, submitting patches and suggestions. The project team is comprised of Members and Contributors. Members have direct access to the source of a project and actively evolve the code-base. Contributors improve the project through submission of patches and suggestions to the Members. The number of Contributors to the project is unbounded. Get involved today. All contributions to the project are greatly appreciated. Members The following is a list of developers with commit privileges that have directly contributed to the project in one way or another. Image Name Email Organization Organization URL IKE Network Team dev@ike.network[1] IKE Network https://ike.network[2] Contributors There are no contributors listed for this project. Please check back again later.

project-info.html.title: Project Information
project-info.html.body: Project Information This document provides an overview of the various documents and links that are part of this project's general information. All of this content is automatically generated by Maven[1] on behalf of the project. Overview Document Description Dependencies[2] This document lists the project's dependencies and provides information on each dependency. Maven Coordinates[3] This document describes how to include this project as a dependency using various dependency management tools. Distribution Management[4] This document provides informations on the distribution management of this project. About[5] Maven 4 build extension that prunes non-existent <subprojects> entries from workspace POMs before model validation. Lets a fresh clone of an IKE workspace bootstrap with mvn ws:scaffold-init before any subproject directory is on disk. Licenses[6] This document lists the project license(s). Plugins[7] This document lists the build plugins and the report plugins used by this project. Source Code Management[8] This document lists ways to access the online source repository. Summary[9] This document lists other related information of this project Team[10] This document provides information on the members of this project. These are the individuals who have contributed to the project in one form or another.
